empirical.interpretation: The open Vidyut Paninian engine (ADR-0010), adopted after the proprietary Samsadhani proved non-redistributable, generates tinanta forms with a form type-token ratio of 0.9613 over 594 forms, exercising 132 distinct Astadhyayi sutras at a mean derivation length of 26.3 steps. This clears the pre-registered 0.80 non-degeneracy threshold from phase-1.yaml: the generator does not collapse into repeated forms and supplies genuine sutra-by-sutra structural annotations for H1. Scope is deliberately generator-level; sequence-level corpus diversity is deferred to the Phase 2 assembly gate, where the DCS corpus (Apache-2.0) provides a real-text entropy floor of 0.98/0.99 bigram/trigram.

integrity.tarka_memo: See docs/contracts/phase-1-tarka-memo.md. Strongest objection: form-level TTR (0.96) measures the wrong granularity vs sequence-level entropy (0.0 on isolated forms); attractor-flow independently flagged an OSCILLATING 2-period regime between the two readings. Resolved via TRIZ P35 (Parameter Changes) + break-symmetry: each metric is bound to the gate it governs (form-level -> Phase 1 generator non-degeneracy; sequence-level -> Phase 2 corpus assembly with DCS floor). Secondary objections (sample size, threshold gaming, Samsadhani->Vidyut faithfulness) examined and found non-fatal. No corpus-level diversity claim is made in Phase 1.
